clues: A user logged into their Windows machine and found all desktop icons, taskbar pins, and personal settings missing.
The user's files are still present in 'C:\Users\[username]\Documents' and other user folders.
The computer presents the user with a 'default' desktop environment as if they have never logged in before.
A temporary profile message was displayed during login due to a corrupt user profile.
The Windows registry hive for the user's profile (NTUSER.DAT) has become corrupted.
